title: solidago rugosa var. typica
intro: Solidago rugosa var. typica, also known as the rough-leaved goldenrod, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is a shrub-like perennial herb native to eastern Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. Solidago rugosa var. typica can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders.
